content.problemStatement:
You're given three non-decreasing arrays AAA, BBB, CCC of length NAN_ANA‚Äč, NBN_BNB‚Äč, NCN_CNC‚Äč. We define,

f(i,j,k)=max‚Ā°(‚ą£Ai‚ąíBj‚ą£,‚ą£Bj‚ąíCk‚ą£,‚ą£Ai‚ąíCk‚ą£)f(i, j, k) = \max{(|A_i - B_j|, |B_j - C_k|, |A_i - C_k|)}f(i,j,k)=max(‚ą£Ai‚Äč‚ąíBj‚Äč‚ą£,‚ą£Bj‚Äč‚ąíCk‚Äč‚ą£,‚ą£Ai‚Äč‚ąíCk‚Äč‚ą£) where 1‚Č§i‚Č§NA,1‚Č§j‚Č§NB,1‚Č§k‚Č§NC1 \leq i \leq N_A, 1 \leq j \leq N_B, 1 \leq k \leq N_C1‚Č§i‚Č§NA‚Äč,1‚Č§j‚Č§NB‚Äč,1‚Č§k‚Č§NC‚Äč

Find the **minimum** possible value of f(i,j,k)f(i, j, k)f(i,j,k) over all possible value of iii, jjj, kkk.

Note: Array AAA being non-decreasing means that A1‚Č§A2‚Č§‚čĮ‚Č§ANA_1 \leq A_2 \leq \dots \leq A_NA1‚Äč‚Č§A2‚Äč‚Č§‚čĮ‚Č§AN‚Äč.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers NAN_ANA‚Äč, NBN_BNB‚Äč, NCN_CNC‚Äč ‚ÄĒ the number of elements in the array AAA, BBB, CCC.
The second line of each test case contains NAN_ANA‚Äč space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANAA_1,A_2,\ldots,A_{N_A}A1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,ANA‚Äč‚Äč ‚ÄĒ the elements of array AAA.
The third line of each test case contains NBN_BNB‚Äč space-separated integers B1,B2,‚Ä¶,BNBB_1,B_2,\ldots,B_{N_B}B1‚Äč,B2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,BNB‚Äč‚Äč ‚ÄĒ the elements of array BBB.
The fourth line of each test case contains NCN_CNC‚Äč space-separated integers C1,C2,‚Ä¶,CNCC_1,C_2,\ldots,C_{N_C}C1‚Äč,C2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,CNC‚Äč‚Äč ‚ÄĒ the elements of array CCC.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the **minimum** possible value of f(i,j,k)f(i, j, k)f(i,j,k).
